test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) or browsing as a guest-capable session.
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the Dashboard heading is visible and authenticated user navigation (account menu) is shown in the top navigation

test_cases.test_case: Invalid credentials show inline error, clear Password and retain Username
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) or browsing as a guest-capable session.
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Enter <invalid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
4. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed near the login form indicating invalid credentials; the Password field is cleared and the Username field still displays the previously entered value

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest grants unauthenticated browsing access
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) or browsing as a guest-capable session.
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Click the Access as a guest button
test_cases.expected_result: The application enters guest browsing mode: browsing content (such as course listings or public pages) is displayed and authenticated account controls are not shown, with a visible indicator that the session is guest access

test_cases.test_case: Cookies notice displays cookie usage information
preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out) or browsing as a guest-capable session.
steps: 1. Navigate to the Login page
2. Click the Cookies notice button
test_cases.expected_result: A cookies information dialog or panel appears containing details about cookie usage

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Username left blank (required text field)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error appears on the Username field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Password left blank (required password field)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline validation error appears on the Password field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; the Username field retains its value; the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on the Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt Log in with invalid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Enter <invalid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error indicating the credentials are invalid is shown; the form does not submit; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains its entered value; the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use Log in when already authenticated (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is authenticated
User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: The Log in control is not available for use when the user is authenticated (it is either not visible or is disabled); clicking it does not submit the form or initiate a login attempt

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Access as a guest' while authenticated (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is authenticated
User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the Access as a guest button
test_cases.expected_result: The Access as a guest control is not available when the user is authenticated (it is either not visible or is disabled); clicking it does not grant guest browsing or navigate away from the current authenticated session

test_cases.test_case: Click disabled 'Lost password?' link on test site
preconditions: User is on the Login page
User is not authenticated
The Lost password? link is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Click the Lost password? link
test_cases.expected_result: The Lost password? link is visibly disabled and is not actionable; clicking it does not navigate or open a password recovery flow and the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Very long Username entered then submit with invalid credentials — retention of full value
preconditions: User is not authenticated and is on the Login page
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an invalid password into the Password field
5. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Log in is blocked; an inline error message is shown; the Password field is cleared (visible empty); the Username field remains populated and visibly retains the full long string that was entered

test_cases.test_case: Username entered with leading and trailing whitespace then submit — trimming behaviour on failure
preconditions: User is not authenticated and is on the Login page
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter the correct username value surrounded by leading and trailing whitespace into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an invalid password into the Password field
5. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Log in is blocked; an inline error message is shown; the Password field is cleared (visible empty); the Username field is retained and visibly shows the username with leading/trailing whitespace trimmed (no leading/trailing spaces visible)

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password containing special characters and emoji submitted as credentials
preconditions: User is not authenticated and is on the Login page
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a username containing special characters and emoji into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter a password containing special characters and emoji into the Password field
5. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Log in is blocked; an inline error message is shown; the Password field is cleared (visible empty); the Username field is retained and visibly shows the exact entered value including the special characters/emoji

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Log in with invalid credentials to check duplicate submissions are prevented
preconditions: User is not authenticated and is on the Login page
steps: 1. Focus the Username field
2. Enter a valid-looking username into the Username field
3. Focus the Password field
4. Enter an invalid password into the Password field
5. Rapidly click the Log in button twice (two quick clicks)
6. Observe the page after the clicks
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked; only a single inline error message is visible; the Password field is cleared (visible empty) after the action; the Username field remains populated with the entered value (no duplicate navigation or duplicate success occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Open Full calendar view from Dashboard
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Full calendar' link
test_cases.expected_result: The Full calendar view opens showing the calendar month grid, the month and year heading, and calendar navigation controls

test_cases.test_case: Open Import or export calendars page from Dashboard
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Import or export calendars' link
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar import/export page opens showing import and export options for calendar data

test_cases.test_case: Start creating a new personal event from Calendar block
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'New event' button
test_cases.expected_result: The event creation interface is displayed (event creation dialog or page with fields to enter event title, date/time, and other details)

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to previous month in Calendar block
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Prev_Month' (left arrow) button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to show the previous month and year and the calendar grid displays the dates for that previous month

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to next month in Calendar block
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Next_Month' (right arrow) button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar heading updates to show the next month and year and the calendar grid displays the dates for that next month

test_cases.test_case: Change Timeline time range filter and view filtered activities
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, select <time range> from the Time_Range dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only activities within the selected <time range>; activities outside that range are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Change Timeline sort order and observe resorted list
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, select <sort order> from the Sort_Order dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline items are resorted according to the selected <sort order>; the visible item order updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Search Timeline items by name or type
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, enter <search query> in the Search field and submit
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays only items matching <search query>; unrelated items are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Enable Edit mode and reveal edit controls
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Toggle the Edit_Mode_Toggle to ON
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode is enabled; the 'Reset page to default' button and '+ Add a block' button are visible and each block displays a move icon and a three-dot block menu

test_cases.test_case: Disable Edit mode and hide edit controls
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Toggle the Edit_Mode_Toggle to OFF
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode is disabled; the 'Reset page to default' and '+ Add a block' buttons are no longer visible and move icons and block menus are hidden from blocks

test_cases.test_case: Add a block from '+ Add a block' while in Edit mode
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the '+ Add a block' button
test_cases.expected_result: The block library page opens listing available block types that can be added to the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Reset Dashboard layout to default while in Edit mode
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
Dashboard has a non-default layout
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click the 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard layout reverts to the default arrangement; blocks are positioned in their default order and any custom additions are no longer present

test_cases.test_case: Open Configure for Timeline block from block menu
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Configure'
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block configuration dialog or panel opens showing configuration options for the Timeline block

test_cases.test_case: Initiate Move for Timeline block via block menu
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Move'
test_cases.expected_result: Move mode is activated for the Timeline block and placeholder drop positions are shown on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Delete the Timeline block via block menu while in Edit mode
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
Timeline block present on Dashboard
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Delete'
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block is removed from the Dashboard and is no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Configure for Calendar block from block menu
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Configure'
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block configuration dialog or panel opens showing configuration options for the Calendar block

test_cases.test_case: Initiate Move for Calendar block via block menu
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Move'
test_cases.expected_result: Move mode is activated for the Calendar block and placeholder drop positions are shown on the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: Delete the Calendar block via block menu while in Edit mode
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
Calendar block present on Dashboard
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Calendar block, open the block menu (three-dot menu) and click 'Delete'
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block is removed from the Dashboard and is no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Initiate drag move of the Timeline block using the move icon
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
Timeline block present on Dashboard
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click and hold the Timeline block's move icon and begin dragging the block (do not complete drop)
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline block's drag handle is engaged and a placeholder indicating possible drop positions is visible as the block follows the cursor

test_cases.test_case: Initiate drag move of the Calendar block using the move icon
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Edit mode enabled
Calendar block present on Dashboard
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. Click and hold the Calendar block's move icon and begin dragging the block (do not complete drop)
test_cases.expected_result: The Calendar block's drag handle is engaged and a placeholder indicating possible drop positions is visible as the block follows the cursor

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows empty state when no activities exist in selected time range
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page
2. In the Timeline block, select a <time range> known to contain no activities (e.g., a future range) from the Time_Range dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: An empty-state message is shown in the Timeline block indicating there are no upcoming activities in the selected range

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Dashboard page
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (not logged in)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Dashboard page URL
test_cases.expected_result: User is not shown the Dashboard. The application redirects to the login page or shows the login prompt; no Dashboard content (Timeline, Calendar, edit controls) is visible.

test_cases.test_case: '+ Add a block' action is not available when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode_Toggle is OFF
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged in and open the Dashboard
2. Verify Edit mode is OFF (Edit_Mode_Toggle unchecked)
3. Attempt to find and click the '+ Add a block' button
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' button is not present/visible on the Dashboard when Edit mode is OFF; the user cannot click it and cannot open the block library from the Dashboard; the Dashboard remains on the same view.

test_cases.test_case: Block-level menu actions (Configure) are not available when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard is open
Edit_Mode_Toggle is OFF
Timeline block is present on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Ensure the user is logged in and open the Dashboard
2. Verify Edit mode is OFF (Edit_Mode_Toggle unchecked)
3. Inspect the Timeline block area for the three-dot Block_Menu or Move_Icon
4. Attempt to open the block menu or click Configure for the Timeline block
test_cases.expected_result: The block-level three-dot menu and Move icon are not visible on the Timeline block when Edit mode is OFF; the user cannot open a Configure action from the Dashboard and no configuration UI appears.

test_cases.test_case: Search field with a very long query (200+ characters)
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard page is loaded and Timeline block is visible
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a very long string of 200+ characters into the Search field (no special characters required)
3. Press Enter or click the Timeline Search submit icon
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Search executes; the Timeline updates and displays results matching the long query. If there are no matches the Timeline shows the empty-state message. No inline error is shown for query length.

test_cases.test_case: Search field with emoji and special/unicode characters
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard page is loaded and Timeline block is visible
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a query containing emoji and a mix of special/unicode characters into the Search field
3. Press Enter or click the Timeline Search submit icon
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Search accepts the emoji/unicode query; the Timeline updates to show items matching the characters if any exist, otherwise the empty-state message is shown. No generic validation error is displayed for use of emoji/special characters.

test_cases.test_case: Search field with leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard page is loaded and Timeline block is visible
steps: 1. Focus the Timeline Search field
2. Enter a search string with leading and trailing spaces into the Search field
3. Press Enter or click the Timeline Search submit icon
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Search executes using the trimmed query; Timeline updates according to the trimmed value (matching items displayed or the empty-state message if none). The UI does not retain visible leading/trailing whitespace in any displayed query field value.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid toggle of Edit mode on then off
preconditions: Authenticated (logged-in) user
Dashboard page is loaded
steps: 1. Click the Edit Mode toggle to enable Edit mode
2. Immediately (without waiting for additional UI animation) click the Edit Mode toggle again to disable Edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: On the first click Edit controls (Reset page to default button, + Add a block button, block move icons and menus) become visible; immediately toggling off hides those edit controls. The Dashboard does not remain in an inconsistent state with edit controls partially visible after the second toggle.

test_cases.test_case: Open a course from its card
preconditions: User logged in as a student with an active account and enrollment records for one or more courses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. Click the <target course name> link on its course card
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course's main page opens; the course title and the course tab bar are visible

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the card menu and verify it is pinned to the top
preconditions: User logged in as a student with an active account
At least two enrolled courses are present in the My Courses listing
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. On the <target course card>, click the three-dot menu
3. Click 'Star this course' from the card menu
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course card appears as the first card in the listing (pinned to the top)

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view using the card menu
preconditions: User logged in as a student with an active account
At least one enrolled course is visible in the My Courses listing
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. On the <target course card>, click the three-dot menu
3. Click 'Remove from view' from the card menu
test_cases.expected_result: The removed course card is no longer visible in the listing; other course cards remain visible

test_cases.test_case: Search for courses using the Course Search field
preconditions: User logged in as a student with an active account
At least two enrolled courses with distinct names exist, one matching <search term> and one not matching
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. Enter <search term> in the Course Search field and submit the search
test_cases.expected_result: Only course cards with names matching <search term> are shown; unrelated course cards are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Change the course listing layout to List
preconditions: User logged in as a student with an active account and enrollment records for one or more courses
steps: 1. Navigate to the My Courses page via the top navigation
2. Open the Layout dropdown control
3. Select 'List' from the Layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The course listing updates to List layout; courses are presented in list rows and each row includes the course name link

test_cases.test_case: Open course card while not authenticated (unauthenticated access blocked)
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. In a browser session where no user is logged in, navigate to the My Courses URL or click the My Courses top-nav link.
2. If the My Courses page loads, click a course card's course name link.
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user is redirected to the login page; the My Courses card grid content and course link are not displayed. The course main page is not reached.

test_cases.test_case: Star a course while logged in as a student with an inactive account (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student whose account status is inactive
There is at least one course card visible for that account
steps: 1. As the inactive student, navigate to the My Courses page.
2. On a course card, open the three-dot menu.
3. Click the 'Star this course' action.
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: 'Star this course' does not pin the course to the top. The course remains in its original position and the UI shows a visible blocking indication — either the 'Star this course' option is disabled or an inline validation error on the action menu indicating an active student account is required. No navigation or pinning occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view while logged in as a student with an inactive account (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student whose account status is inactive
There is at least one course card visible for that account
steps: 1. As the inactive student, navigate to the My Courses page.
2. On a course card, open the three-dot menu.
3. Click the 'Remove from view' action.
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked: the course is not hidden from the view. The course card remains visible in the grid and an inline indication appears on the action menu that an active student account is required (or the menu option is disabled). No course hiding occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Access My Courses while logged in as a non-student role (access/visibility restricted)
preconditions: User is logged in with a non-student role (e.g., instructor or admin)
steps: 1. While logged in as the non-student role, inspect the top navigation bar for the My Courses link.
2. Attempt to navigate to the My Courses URL directly.
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked or restricted: the My Courses link is not visible in the top navigation bar for this role, and navigating directly to the My Courses URL does not display course cards — instead the page shows an access-restricted/empty state and no course card actions are available.

test_cases.test_case: Open course card while logged in as a student with an inactive account (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student whose account status is inactive
There is at least one course card visible for that account
steps: 1. As the inactive student, navigate to the My Courses page.
2. Click the course name link on a course card to open the course.
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: clicking the course name does not navigate to the course main page. The user remains on My Courses and an inline blocking indication appears (e.g., the course link is disabled or an inline message indicates an active student account is required). No course main page is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query in Course_Search
preconditions: Student is logged in
My Courses page is open
steps: 1. Focus the Course_Search field
2. Type a very long string (200+ characters) into Course_Search
3. Press Enter or click the search icon to run the search
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: the Course_Search field retains the full entered string; no inline error is shown; the course grid refreshes and displays either matching course cards or a visible 'no results' message

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click on 'Star this course' (idempotent pin action)
preconditions: Student is logged in
At least one enrolled course card is visible in the grid
steps: 1. Open the three-dot menu on a visible course card
2. Click 'Star this course'
3. Immediately click 'Star this course' again before the UI finishes updating
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: the course is shown in the starred state and pinned to the top of the grid exactly once; the UI shows the starred state on the card and no duplicate card entries or duplicate pins are introduced

test_cases.test_case: Course_Search with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed for matching
preconditions: Student is logged in
My Courses page is open
There exists at least one enrolled course whose name contains the substring <search_term>
steps: 1. Focus the Course_Search field
2. Type the search value with leading and trailing spaces, e.g. '  <search_term>  '
3. Press Enter or click the search icon to run the search
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: leading and trailing whitespace are trimmed for matching; the course grid displays the course(s) containing <search_term>; the Course_Search field displays the trimmed value (without the leading/trailing spaces)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Course_Name link to open course
preconditions: Student is logged in
At least one enrolled course card is visible in the grid using the current filters/layout
steps: 1. Click the Course_Name link on a visible course card
2. Immediately click the same Course_Name link again before navigation completes
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: navigation completes to the course main page once and the course main page is displayed; no UI error is shown and the current tab does not open duplicate course pages

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view then view under Status_Filter = Hidden
preconditions: Student is logged in
At least one enrolled course card is visible in the grid using the current filters/layout
steps: 1. Open the three-dot menu on a visible course card
2. Click 'Remove from view' for that course
3. Open the Status_Filter dropdown
4. Select the 'Hidden' option in Status_Filter
5. Observe the course grid
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: the course removed from view appears in the grid when Status_Filter is set to Hidden; when Status_Filter is set to any option other than Hidden, the removed course is not shown in the grid

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Course tab from Course Page tab bar
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
steps: 1. Click the 'Course' tab in the horizontal tab bar
test_cases.expected_result: Navigated to Course tab; course tab content displayed

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Participants tab from Course Page tab bar
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
steps: 1. Click the 'Participants' tab in the horizontal tab bar
test_cases.expected_result: Navigated to Participants tab; participants list displayed

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Grades tab from Course Page tab bar
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
steps: 1. Click the 'Grades' tab in the horizontal tab bar
test_cases.expected_result: Navigated to Grades tab; grades content displayed

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Activities tab from Course Page tab bar
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
steps: 1. Click the 'Activities' tab in the horizontal tab bar
test_cases.expected_result: Navigated to Activities tab; activities content displayed

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Competencies tab from Course Page tab bar
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
steps: 1. Click the 'Competencies' tab in the horizontal tab bar
test_cases.expected_result: Navigated to Competencies tab; competencies content displayed

test_cases.test_case: Collapse all sections using the 'Collapse all' link
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
<Course Page> has multiple sections expanded
steps: 1. Click the 'Collapse all' link in the course action bar
test_cases.expected_result: All sections are collapsed; section contents are hidden and only section headers with collapsed chevrons are visible

test_cases.test_case: Expand a collapsed section by clicking its chevron toggle
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
The <Section> is currently collapsed
steps: 1. Click the chevron toggle for the <Section>
test_cases.expected_result: The <Section> expands, showing its list of activities and resources with type icons and clickable names

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a section open by clicking its section name link
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
The <Section> is currently collapsed
steps: 1. Click the <Section> name link in the section header
test_cases.expected_result: The <Section> expands, showing its list of activities and resources with type icons and clickable names

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity from an expanded section by clicking the activity name
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
The <Section> is expanded and contains at least one <Activity>
steps: 1. Click the activity name <Activity> listed inside the <Section>
test_cases.expected_result: The <Activity> page opens, showing its title and content

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity via its clickable name (alternate repeating-group item)
preconditions: User logged in as <Enrolled user> and has the <Course Page> open
The <Section> is expanded and contains at least one <Activity>
steps: 1. Click the activity name link for the alternate <Activity> item inside the <Section>

3. Observe the resulting page shown to the user

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Log out button

test_cases.expected_result: The first click terminates the session and redirects to the login page; the second rapid click does not produce an error and is ignored. Logout succeeds and the login page is visible.

test_cases.test_case: Click Log out then immediately navigate to a protected page via top navigation
preconditions: User is logged in and currently viewing a protected page
steps: 1. Click the initials icon in the top navigation bar to open the user menu dropdown
2. Click the Log out button
3. Immediately (before the login page finishes rendering) click a top-navigation link that points to another protected page
test_cases.expected_result: The attempt to view the protected page after logout is blocked; the login page is shown. Access is blocked and a re-authentication prompt (login page) is visible.

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back button immediately after logout

steps: 1. Click the initials icon in the top navigation bar to open the user menu dropdown
2. Click the Log out button
3. After the app redirects to the login page, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Using the browser Back button does not reveal protected content; navigation is blocked and the login page remains visible (protected pages are not displayed). Access is blocked.

test_cases.test_case: Manually enter a protected page URL after logout
preconditions: User is logged in and currently viewing a protected page
steps: 1. Click the initials icon in the top navigation bar to open the user menu dropdown
2. Click the Log out button
3. After being redirected to the login page, type a known protected page URL into the browser address bar and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Direct navigation to the protected URL is blocked; the login page is shown and the protected page content is not displayed. Access is blocked.

module_context.summary: Provides a form for users to authenticate with Username and Password, offers guest access and cookie information, and displays inline errors on invalid credentials.
module_context.where_it_fits: Serves as the application's primary authentication entry point reached from the Home page and other public links; successful authentication redirects the user to the Dashboard, and the Log out action returns users to this page. It precedes access to course-specific navigation (Dashboard, My Courses, Course Page) and other authenticated modules.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is not authenticated (logged out) or browsing as a guest-capable session.

module_context.summary: The Dashboard presents a personalized landing page with a Timeline of upcoming activities and a Calendar view for events across the user’s courses, plus edit-mode controls for adding and configuring blocks.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar as the Dashboard link and serving as the user’s primary landing/overview screen after login, it aggregates course activity and calendar data and links to the full calendar and calendar import/export pages. It complements course-level views (Course Page, Activities) by providing cross-course timelines and quick access to calendar management in the application lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated (logged-in) user; user context loaded (profile and enrolled courses information available)

module_context.summary: Displays all courses in which the student is enrolled as visual cards with controls for filtering, searching, sorting, layout, and quick actions (star or hide).
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the My Courses link (and reachable from Home or Dashboard), this module is the student-facing course listing page that precedes entering a specific Course Page; clicking a course card navigates to that course's main page where the course tab bar and breadcrumbs are shown.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a student with an active account and enrollment records for one or more courses (or none).

module_context.summary: Displays the full course content organized into collapsible sections that list activities and resources with type icons and clickable names.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation (Home → Dashboard → My Courses) when a user opens a specific course, the Course Page appears beneath the course title with the horizontal tab bar (Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, Competencies) and is used during course viewing alongside the Course Index sidebar.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged-in user who is enrolled in and has opened a specific course

module_context.summary: Displays a searchable and filterable list of users enrolled in the current course, showing names, roles, groups, and last access information.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from within a course via the horizontal tab bar (Course → Participants) and linked in the course breadcrumbs and Course Index; it is part of the course-level user management and information views shown while viewing a course. Students see participant listings but not enrollment management options, while instructors use it for reviewing enrolments and accessing profiles.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course page with course context loaded and enrollment data available.

module_context.summary: Displays the student's own course grades in a user report table listing grade items, calculated weights, earned values, ranges, percentages, feedback, and each item's contribution to the course total.
module_context.where_it_fits: Available from within a course via the horizontal tab bar (Home/Dashboard/My Courses → select course → Grades), it is the student-facing view alongside Course, Participants, Activities, and Competencies for reviewing personal grade information rather than the full gradebook. It is part of the course-level navigation and appears when viewing a specific course.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a student and currently viewing a specific course that contains graded activities (permission to view own grades).

module_context.summary: Displays an individual assignment’s details, submission interface, and submission/grading status including dates, description, student submissions, and teacher feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from a Course Page via the Activities tab or the Course Index sidebar when viewing a course; it is the activity-level view where students submit work and later review grades and feedback as part of the course workflow.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a course participant (student) enrolled in the course, viewing the specific course with the assignment published and visible in the course’s activity list.

module_context.summary: Provides a consolidated, type-grouped overview of all course activities with expandable sections and direct links to each activity page.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the course-level horizontal tab bar (Course > Activities) when viewing a specific course, and reachable via the Course Index sidebar or activity breadcrumbs; it sits alongside Participants and Grades as a course navigation hub for locating activities. It is part of the course view lifecycle for browsing and jumping into individual activity pages.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and currently viewing a specific course page with course context and enrollment in that course.

module_context.summary: Displays the student's personal information, activity links, privacy and report summaries, and provides access to an editable profile form with collapsible sections for personal details, picture, and optional fields.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation user menu (Profile) available on every page, the Profile page is part of the user account area alongside Grades, Calendar, and Preferences and is reachable from the home/dashboard context; it complements course-level pages by linking to enrolled course profiles and reporting sections.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user viewing their own profile (logged in as the profile owner)

module_context.summary: Ends the current authenticated session and returns the user to the login page, requiring re-authentication for protected pages.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located in the user menu dropdown accessed from the initials icon in the top navigation bar (right side); it is available from anywhere in the app and acts as the final action in a user's session lifecycle by redirecting to the login page.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: An active authenticated user session (user is logged in and has access to protected pages).
